chunks.text: ORDER SHEET THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI C.P. No.D-3054 of 2020 Date Order with signature of Judge(s) 1. For hearing of CMA No.13071/2020. 2. For hearing of main case. 12.04.2022 Mr. Hassan Ali, advocate for the petitioner. Mr. Shehryar Mahar, Additional Advocate General Sindh. ********** The petition was filed in 2020 seeking the following relief: 1. “Declare any Notice / Challan issued by the Respondents illegal and without jurisdiction; 2. Restrain the Respondents from enforcing any recovery or taking any coercive action against the in respect of recovery of 20 years of Stamp Duty whereas the BPRD Circular provides that the Term Finance Certificates are redeemable after 5 years.” Learned counsel for the petitioner was specifically asked whether there was any order, notice and/or challan was impugned in the present proceedings and he replied in negative. It was further queried as to whether any cause had been demonstrated by the petitioner to have been occasioned in respect whereof remedy was sought, however, learned counsel remained unable to demonstrate the occurrence of any grievance. It is settled law that a court does not decide a lis on mere presumptions or apprehensions but that a cause of action ought to be based on a demonstrable grievance, requiring remedy1. In the present facts and circumstances no cause of action has been articulated before us to merit invocation of the discretionary2 writ jurisdiction of this Court. In view hereof, it is apparent that the present petition is premature, in the very least. This petition is found to be misconceived, hence, hereby dismissed. JUDGE JUDGE Khuhro/PA 1 Per Muhammad Ali Mazhar, J. in Bashir Ahmed Badini & Others vs.

This petition is found to be misconceived, hence, hereby dismissed. JUDGE JUDGE Khuhro/PA 1 Per Muhammad Ali Mazhar, J. in Bashir Ahmed Badini & Others vs. Chairman and Member Administrative Committee High Court Balochistan reported as 2022 SCMR 448; Punjab vs Yasir Majeed Shaikh reported as 2021 SCMR 624; Per Ayesha A Malik J in Muhammad Aslam vs. Pakistan reported as 2017 PTD 803; Per Athar Minallah CJ in Mari Petroleum Company Limited vs. Pakistan reported as 2019 PTD 1774. 2 Per Ijaz Ul Ahsan J. in Syed Iqbal Hussain Shah Gillani vs. PBC & Others reported as 2021 SCMR 425; Muhammad Fiaz Khan vs. Ajmer Khan & Another reported as 2010 SCMR 105.
